title: narcissus pseudonarcissus var. moschatus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus var. moschatus, also known as the sweet-scented daffodil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, the bulbs of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus var. moschatus are said to include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a natural expectorant. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of the plant are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
